calendar.matters:
NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Aug-24-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on calendar today for Elder Adult Abuse OSC and TRO hearing this day.

Dept. 204. Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

Requestor under a durable power of attorney: Stephen Smalley, was present with Counsel, Elizabeth Hansen.

Conservator: Tom Lucas was present, with Counsel, Helene Wenzel.

Litigation Counsel for Beatrice Smalley, Paula Canny, was present.

Respondent: Patrick Dana was not present.

Requestor, Stephen Smalley, was sworn.

The Court hears testimony, receives evidence and after hearing the evidence presented, the Petitioner's order is Granted as follows: Respondent shall stay at least 100 yards away from the protected person: John Patrick Smalley, his Residence, and Beatrice Smalley.

Respondent shall not Physically Abuse, Financially Abuse, Intimidate, Molest, Attack, Assault (sexually or otherwise), Hit, Follow, Stalk, Threaten, Harass, Destroy Personal Property, Keep under Surveillance, or Block Movements, Contact (directly or indirectly), Telephone, Send Messages, Mail or E-Mail.

Ms. Hansen inquired about the order regarding her attorney's fees and costs. The Court directed Ms. Hansen to proceed by way of a noticed motion. Ms. Hansen requested that the rental properties be included in the order. The Court declined to include the rental properties in the stay away order noting that it was not in the original filing.

Expires: August 24, 2014.

Order signed.

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Jun-23-2011 Reset To Aug-24-11 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on calendar today for Elder Adult Abuse OSC and TRO hearing this day.

Dept. 204. Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Court-Appointed Counsel Ernest Der was present for John Patrick Smalley, Conservatee/Person to be Protected.

Elizabeth Hansen, Counsel for Petitioner/Requestor with durable power of attorney Stephen Smalley, was present.

Stephen Smalley was not present.

Respondent: Patrick Dana was not present.

Mr. Der and Ms. Hansen stipulated to Commissioner Sue Kaplan sitting as Judge Pro Tem on this matter.

Matter called. The Court presented a brief history of the matter, noted that the "landscape" in regard to this matter had changed, and addressed the non-appearance of Court-Appointed Counsel for Beatrice Smalley, Grant Tomioka, as well as the non-appearance of the Conservator Tom Lucas, and his Counsel, Helene Wenzel. Ms. Hansen stated that the parties were notified via an email. Discussions regarding the non-appearances were held. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court stated that it wanted to continue this matter to a date past the August 4, 2011 status date currently set in the conservatorships and the Trust matter. The Court contined this matter to August 24, 2011 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 204, extended the TRO to the same date, directed Ms. Hansen to prepare the reissuance, and also requested that Ms. Hansen send notice of the August 24, 2011 hearing to Mr. Tomioka, Mr. Lucas, and Ms. Wenzel. In addition, the Court requested that Ms. Hansen brief the issue of the impact of the conservatorship on the underlying Elder Abuse matter and requested that she have her brief on file by noon on August 19, 2011. The Court stated that all Counsel could brief the issue if they wished to do so.

Reissuance expires: August 24, 2011.

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Apr-20-2011 Reset To Jun-23-11 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Mar-21-2011 Reset To Apr-20-11 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on calendar today for Elder Adult Abuse OSC and TRO hearing this day.

This matter was called with case number PED-10-294158 (Stephen Smalley vs. Charles Theodore Murr).

Dept. 204. Judge: John Dearman. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Court-Appointed Counsel Ernest Der was present for John Patrick Smalley, Conservatee.

Petitioner/Requestor: Stephen Smalley, was present with Counsel, Elizabeth Hansen.

Conservator: Tom Lucas was present, with Counsel, Helene Wenzel.

Respondent: Patrick Dana was present.

Matter called. Ms. Hansen stated that the parties have stipulated to a continuance of this matter to April 20, 2011 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 204. The Court continued the matter and extended the Elder Abuse TRO to April 20, 2011. Mr. Dana stated his address for the record. Ms. Hansen presented her concerns with service and Mr. Dana's address as set forth on the record. Mr. Dana agreed to substituted service by mail.

Reissuance expires: April 20, 2011.

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Jan-26-2011 Reset To Feb-16-11 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: All causes came on calendar today for Elder Adult Abuse OSC and TRO hearing this day.

This matter was called with case numbers: PED-10-294158 (Stephen Smalley vs. Charles Murr) and PCN-10-294149 (Conservatorship of John Patrick Smalley).

Court-Appointed Counsel Ernest Der appeared by telephone for John Patrick Smalley, Conservatee.
Helene Wenzel for and with Thomas Lucas, Temporary Conservator, in case number PCN-10-294149.
Elizabeth Hansen for and with Stephen Smalley, Petitioner in all three matters.
Richard Grabstein for and with Maurice Dolan, Respondent, in case number PCN-10-294149.
John Dooling for Theodore Charles Murr, Respondent in case number PED-10-294158.
Court-Appointed Counsel Grant Tomioka for Beatrice Smalley, was also present.
Patrick Smalley was also present.

Ms. Hansen stated that a reissuance was needed in case number PED-10-294159, Stephen Smalley vs. Patrick Dana, because the Respondent, Patrick Dana, had not been served. Ms. Hansen also stated that she and Mr. Dooling, Counsel for the Respondent, in case number PED-10-294158, Stephen Smalley vs. Charles Theodore Murr, had agreed to a continuance of that matter. The Court continued case numbers PED-10-294158 and PED-10-294159 to February 16, 2011 at 1:30 p.m. in Department 204 and reissued the Elder Abuse TRO's in both matters to February 16, 2011. Both reissuances expire: February 16, 2011 (orders signed).

Ms. Hansen stated that she was ready to proceed with case number PCN-10-294149. The Court hears argument by both Counsel, and after hearing the argument presented, the Petitioner's order is Granted as follows: Respondent shall stay at least 100 yards away from the protected persons: John Patrick Smalley and Beatrice Smalley. No Contact (see order for text). Order expires: 7/26/11.

Dept. 204. Judge: John Dearman. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Jan-12-2011 Reset To Jan-26-11 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: All causes came on calendar today for Elder Adult Abuse OSC and TRO hearing this day.

This matter was called with case numbers: PED-10-294158 (Stephen Smalley vs. Charles Theodore Murr) and PED-10-294159 (Stephen Smalley vs. Patrick Dana).

Dept. 204. Judge: John Dearman. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Court-Appointed Counsel Ernest Der for John Patrick Smalley, Conservatee.

Helene Wenzel for and with Thomas Lucas, Temporary Conservator, in case number PCN-10-294149.

Elizabeth Hansen for and with Stephen Smalley, Petitioner in all three matters.

Richard Grabstein for and with Maurice Dolan, Respondent, in case number PCN-10-294149.

Theodore Charles Murr, Respondent in case number PED-10-294158, was also present.

Patrick Smalley and Christine Smalley So were also present.

Ms. Hansen stated that in the first matter that Patrick Dana had not been served and therefore a reissuance was needed, that in the second matter that Mr. Murr had filed a 170.6 challenge to Commissioner Kaplan and requested a continuance, that her client doesn't oppose Mr. Murr's request for a continuance as long as the temporary restraining order (TRO) is reissued, and that in the third matter that she and Mr. Grabstein had agreed to a continuance. Mr. Grabstein confirmed that they agreed to continue the matter.

The Court continued all three matters to January 26, 2011 and reissued all three Elder Abuse TRO's.

Reissuances expire: January 26, 2011.

Orders signed.
